title: Leucanthemum rohlenae
intro: Leucanthemum rohlenae, also known as Rohlena's daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Czech Republic. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum rohlenae include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leucanthemum rohlenae is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Leucanthemum rohlenae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Leucanthemum, which is a group of daisy-like plants. The genus Leucanthemum is further classified under the order Asterales, which includes other flowering plants like sunflowers and daisies. Within the family Asteraceae, Leucanthemum rohlenae is part of the subfamily Asteroideae, which is a large group of flowering plants. The exact classification of Leucanthemum rohlenae is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Leucanthemum, Species: L. rohlenae.
